title: Cash Basis Accounting: A local company has a cash basis accounting system. They...
summary: Apply cash basis accounting principles where revenue and expenses are recognized upon cash exchange.
prompt: A local company has a cash basis accounting system. They received $20,000 for services provided but had incurred $5,000 in expenses that will be paid next year. How would this affect their net income for the current year?
solution: Step 1: Under cash basis accounting, revenues are recognized when cash is received. Hence, the income recognized is $20,000. Step 2: Expenses are recognized when cash is paid. As a result, the company does not recognize the $5,000 expense until the following year. Step 3: Therefore, net income for the current year is $20,000.
